channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Mittens the cat is mistakenly left on a plane and becomes a frequent flyer
item.description: WELLINGTON, New Zealand (AP) — A Maine coon cat named Mittens became an accidental jetsetter this month when her cage was overlooked in a plane cargo hold and she made three trips in 24 hours between New Zealand and Australia.
item.dc:creator: CHARLOTTE GRAHAM-McLAY, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: A rebel group is advancing on eastern Congo's largest city and over 100,000 people have fled
item.description: NZULO, Congo (AP) — The M23 rebel group's advance toward eastern Congo's largest city has displaced over 178,000 people in the past two weeks, the United Nations said, as the fighters closed in on Goma on the border with Rwanda.
item.dc:creator: JUSTIN KABUMBA, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Middle East latest: Israel says it will keep control of Gaza-Egypt crossing
item.description: Israel said it will maintain control of the Rafah border crossing between Egypt and the Gaza Strip during the first phase of the ceasefire with Hamas.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Previously approved refugee flights to US canceled after Trump order
item.description: The U.S. refugee program is considered one of the most rigorous on the globe, providing a pathway to safe haven for those fleeing danger or who has faced persecution due to their race, religion, nationality or political views.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, Fox 5

item.title: Man stole $10K worth of coats from dry cleaners: Police
item.description: The suspect was last seen walking down Flint Avenue with the coats under his arm, according to police.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Bawden, NewsNation

item.title: Everything you need to know about the 2025 Sundance Film Festival
item.description: PARK CITY, Utah (AP) — As many in the entertainment industry navigate the devastating effects of the California wildfires, some will soon be decamping to cleaner air in the mountains. The annual Sundance Film Festival begins Thursday in Park City, Utah.
item.dc:creator: LINDSEY BAHR, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Historic Deep South snow: The science behind it
item.description: Parts of the Gulf Coast measured a foot of snow on Tuesday. For many cities the totals obliterate long-standing snowfall records.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Berardelli, KRON4

item.title: DOJ to investigate state or local officials who obstruct immigration enforcement: memo
item.description: A new DOJ memo signals that federal prosecutors should launch investigations into state and local officials who obstruct immigration enforcement.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Border Patrol agent killed in Vermont worked at the Pentagon during 9/11, family says
item.description: A U.S. Border Patrol agent who was killed in Vermont during a traffic stop near the Canadian border was a military veteran who worked security duty at the Pentagon during the time of the Sept. 11 attacks, his family said.
item.dc:creator: KATHY McCORMACK and PATRICK WHITTLE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Colorado woman ID'ed as driver in crash that killed officer
item.description: The driver of a vehicle that collided head on with a Navasota police vehicle killing Sgt. Mark Butler has been identified as 59-year-old Kimberly Lannigan, of Colorado Springs, Colorado. She was also killed in the crash.
item.dc:creator: Roland Richter, Fox 5

item.title: California officials reopen certain Palisades Fire zones, cops to use planes to monitor looting
item.description: California officials will reopen some Palisades Fire evacuation zones, as law enforcement ramps up security to address looting.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump open to Elon Musk buying TikTok
item.description: TikTok restored service to U.S. users Sunday afternoon and credited Trump for pushing to keep the app operational.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, NewsNation

item.title: Trump set to visit NC, California, Nevada
item.description: The president said he will be going Friday to North Carolina, which he said “has been abandoned by the Democrats.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: Trump fires heads of TSA, Coast Guard and guts key aviation safety advisory committee
item.description: President Donald Trump moved quickly to remake the Department of Homeland Security Tuesday, firing the heads of the Transportation Security Administration and Coast Guard before their terms are up and eliminated all the members of a key aviation security advisory group.
item.dc:creator: JOSH FUNK, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Guitarist John Sykes of Whitesnake, Thin Lizzy dies
item.description: "In his final days, he spoke of his sincere love and gratitude for his fans who stuck by him through all these years," a statement released Monday read.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, WFLA, NBC

item.title: President Trump wants to make showerheads and toilets flow greatly again, but so may utility bills
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump once expressed concern with how low showerhead flow affected his “perfect” hair. Now back in the White House, he's again taking aim at some high-efficiency household items — and that may mean higher water and electric bills in your home.
item.dc:creator: ALEXA ST. JOHN and SETH BORENSTEIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump rolls back Biden directive to study methods of lowering prescription costs
item.description: Among the dozens of Biden-era executive orders he rescinded on day one, President Trump nixed one that aimed to explore new health care payment models that would lower prescription costs for Medicare and Medicaid beneficiaries.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Choi, KRON4

item.title: Ex-girlfriend of brother accused in gold-digging plot says she can’t afford attorney
item.description: Las Vegas Metro police said Jennifer Courtemanche plotted with her friend to steal nearly $445,000 from Gavin Maloof.
item.dc:creator: Vanessa Murphy, NewsNation

item.title: First female Coast Guard commandant ousted by Trump administration with no explanation
item.description: Coast Guard Commandant Adm. Linda Fagan, the first female uniformed leader of an Armed Forces branch in U.S. history, was ousted by the Trump administration with no explanation late Monday.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, KTLA 5

item.title: Trump administration ousts Linda Fagan, first female Coast Guard commandant
item.description: Adm. Kevin Lunday, the former vice commandant of the Coast Guard, will take over as acting commandant, the statement notes, offering no other details of the switch.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, NewsNation

item.title: Trump wants to pull the US out of the World Health Organization again. Here's what may happen next
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump used one of the flurry of executive actions that he issued on his first day back in the White House to begin the process of withdrawing the U.S.
item.dc:creator: MARIA CHENG and WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Chicago police reminded they can't cooperate with deportations
item.description: It's not just Chicago police policy but also state law that prohibits officers in Illinois from coordinating with immigration agents or even inquiring about a person's immigration status.
item.dc:creator: Ben Bradley, NewsNation

item.title: Trump wants to pull US out of World Health Organization again; here's what may happen next
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — U.S. President Donald Trump used one of the flurry of executive actions that he issued on his first day back in the White House to begin the process of withdrawing the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Maria Cheng, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Nose spray to treat depression? FDA approves first-of-its-kind medicine
item.description: The U.S. Food and Drug Administration has approved a nose spray to help treat depression, Johnson & Johnson announced Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, KRON4

item.title: Deep South hit with rare winter storm; historic snowfall expected
item.description: Millions of Americans face an artic blast, including the first-ever blizzard warning for parts of the Gulf Coast.
item.dc:creator: Xavier Walton, Fox 5

item.title: What exactly is an executive order? Examining Trump's tool for quickly reshaping government
item.description: Donald Trump returned to the White House ready to immediately overhaul the government using the fastest tool he has — the executive order.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Democrat congressman condemns 'what appears to be a Heil Hitler salute' from Musk
item.description: Rep. Jerry Nadler (D-N.Y.) was among those who sharply criticized billionaire Elon Musk on Monday for making "what appears to be a Heil Hitler salute" during an event celebrating the inauguration of President Trump.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, ABC 4

item.title: Trump rolls out his blueprint on border security, but his orders will face challenges
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump rolled out a blueprint to beef up security at the southern border in a series of executive orders that began taking effect soon after his inauguration Monday, making good on his defining political promise to crack down on immigration and marking another wild
item.dc:creator: REBECCA SANTANA, GISELA SALOMON and ELLIOT SPAGAT, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump pardons nearly all Jan. 6 rioters on first day of presidency
item.description: President Trump pardoned nearly all Jan. 6 defendants on Monday, making good on a long-standing campaign promise just hours into his second presidency.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Senate confirms Marco Rubio as secretary of state, giving Trump the first member of his Cabinet
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Senate quickly confirmed Marco Rubio as secretary of state Monday, voting unanimously to give President Donald Trump the first member of his new Cabinet on Inauguration Day.
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump signs executive orders on federal workforce, free speech
item.description: President Trump signed multiple executive orders during his inauguration parade at Capital One Arena on Monday evening, throwing pens to supporters after a signing ceremony. The signings at a small desk set up on stage in the Washington, D.C.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Republicans look to probe some who received last-day Biden pardons
item.description: Congressional Republicans are looking to press on in scrutinizing some of those who received pardons from former President Biden in the last hours of his presidency. “Implication is that they needed the pardons… So, let’s call them all before Congress and demand the truth,” Rep.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, WGN 9

item.title: The rise
item.description: The possibility of the U.S. outlawing TikTok kept influencers and users in anxious limbo during the four-plus years that lawmakers and judges debated the fate of the video-sharing platform. The moment its fans dreaded arrived late Saturday when the platform went dark in response to a federal ban.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, ABC 4
